refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall update the worksite model within the customer-required timeliness for operational use.
refined_fr.rationale: The candidate FR specifies a single functional obligation: timely updating of the worksite model according to customer requirements. It does not combine multiple functions or include implementation details, thus it is atomic and solution-neutral.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure the latency of worksite model updates under supported scenarios and verify that the 95th percentile latency (p95) meets or is below the customer-defined target (e.g., ≤ 1.0 second).
sub_frs: The NGHE solution shall update the worksite model within the customer-required timeliness for operational use.

candidate_dps: Incremental update mechanism that processes only changed data to minimize latency
Batch update mechanism that processes full worksite model at fixed intervals
Event-driven update mechanism triggered by detected changes in the worksite environment
Parallel processing architecture that divides the worksite model into segments updated concurrently
Prioritization scheme that updates critical worksite areas first to meet timeliness requirements
selected_dp.selected_dp: Incremental update mechanism that processes only changed data to minimize latency

potential_sources: Integration with safety plan and operational policy modules
coupling_check.notes: The incremental update DP supports independence by focusing on changed data only, reducing unnecessary processing and helping meet latency targets. Potential coupling may arise from dependencies on data sources and policy modules, which should be managed via clear interfaces.

assumptions_risks: Assumes that changes in the worksite model can be detected efficiently and accurately
Risk that incremental updates may miss some changes if detection is imperfect
Assumes that the system can handle variable update loads without violating latency constraints
Risk that integration with safety and operational policies may introduce delays or complexity
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider explicitly defining interfaces and protocols for change detection and integration with safety and policy modules to ensure modularity and maintainability.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution module encompasses the core data processing and update mechanisms for the system.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives data updates and sends processed incremental updates.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Handles communication of incremental updates to other system components.

module_containment_scores.notes: The incremental update mechanism logically fits within NGHE Solution as it handles data processing; minimal diffusion expected outside this module.
Vehicle module is more focused on physical control and state; incremental data updates would require significant diffusion to other modules.
Teleoperation focuses on remote control commands rather than data processing; containment is weak.
Safety Services is specialized for safety alerts and monitoring, not data update processing; poor containment.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The incremental update mechanism is best contained within the NGHE Solution module, which is responsible for core data processing and update logic, minimizing diffusion and maintaining cohesion.
containment_risks: Potential interface complexity with Telemetry and Network Link may cause some coupling outside NGHE Solution.
If incremental updates require real-time safety validation, some diffusion to Safety Services might occur.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: If containment risks increase, consider refining the DP to explicitly separate data processing from safety validation to improve modular boundaries.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP of an incremental update mechanism that processes only changed data directly addresses the FR of updating the worksite model within the customer-required timeliness by minimizing latency. This logical solution concept aligns well with the functional requirement and supports meeting the acceptance criteria of update latency p95 ≤ 1.0 s.

diffusion_risks: Potential interface complexity with Telemetry and Network Link may cause some coupling outside NGHE Solution.
If incremental updates require real-time safety validation, some diffusion to Safety Services might occur.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The incremental update mechanism is well contained within the NGHE Solution module, which has a high containment score (8) and low diffusion score (2). Other modules show poor containment and higher diffusion scores, indicating minimal scattering of the DP across multiple IMs. However, some coupling risks exist due to external interfaces and possible safety validation needs.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification of the FR through the IM is feasible by measuring update latency metrics within the NGHE Solution module and validating data flows through the Telemetry and Network Link interfaces.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The ownership candidate NGHE Solution clearly encapsulates the DP within a single module boundary, providing clear allocation and minimizing ambiguity in responsibility.

recommended_revisions: If containment risks increase, refine the DP to explicitly separate data processing from safety validation to improve modular boundaries and reduce diffusion.
Monitor interface complexity with Telemetry and Network Link to ensure coupling does not degrade modularity.
Consider defining explicit interface contracts to manage dependencies and maintain clear separation between NGHE Solution and other modules.

decision.justification: All gates (FR->DP, DP->IM, FR->IM) have passed successfully, indicating that the current level of decomposition sufficiently addresses the functional requirement with a well-contained design parameter and clear implementation module ownership. The incremental update mechanism meets the latency requirements and is well encapsulated within the NGHE Solution module, with manageable diffusion risks. Given the high safety criticality, the current design includes considerations for safety validation and interface complexity, with recommended monitoring and refinement as needed. Therefore, further decomposition is not necessary at this stage.

revise.why: If containment risks increase or interface complexity worsens, refine the DP to explicitly separate data processing from safety validation to improve modular boundaries and reduce diffusion.

assumptions: The incremental update mechanism sufficiently meets the update latency requirement (p95 ≤ 1.0 s).
The NGHE Solution module has high containment and low diffusion scores, indicating good modularity.
Safety validation needs are currently manageable within the existing module boundaries.
open_risks: Potential interface complexity with Telemetry and Network Link may cause coupling outside NGHE Solution.
Possible diffusion to Safety Services if real-time safety validation is required.
evidence_needed: Monitoring data on interface complexity and coupling over time.
Verification metrics of update latency within NGHE Solution module.
Validation of data flows through Telemetry and Network Link interfaces.
